text: Nse otii owo alɛ ɔkpaa fɔ ankyɛnko kakankɔ anfũ fɔ awu ya fankpee fantɛ ni kasɔ yi, faadiki osi ale kafɔɔ fatɔɔ.
translated: The man and the woman went to the forest to cut sticks to build a fence around their farm, but the man's knife was blunt.

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Obe wɔ di Yesu ladie ninfa oosifi nii, banumbiɛtɛ banyɔ banwii lataka baatikanko nwɔ, baatooyiisa kudu baatoobuɛ alɛ, “Ɔka Dawid Obi ee! Nya wo nyaami.”
translated: Some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, "Teacher, rebuke your disciples!" "I tell you," he replied, "if they keep quiet, the stones will cry out." As he approached Jerusalem and saw the city, he wept over it and said, "If you, even you, had only known on this day what would bring you peace—but now it is hidden from your eyes."

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Obe wɔ boole ni alesaa nwu nkpo, Yesu latɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Nwaako kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ otii onwii kaamɛ lee madiki mi anta.”
translated: When he came to the other side, a great crowd welcomed him, Jesus saw them and said, "Teacher, I have compassion for these people because they have stayed with me three days."

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Nwɔ mmle ninle kpɛmblatɛ nii wɔ lediki ni nɛ. Mbɔmbɔ nwɔ, obuo mi anu nwaa kafɔɔ. Mayɔ Ninwuna nii nkpee nwɔ kaamɛ alɛ abadiki lɛkakanko nii ambuɛ antɔkɔ nnle mmuu.
translated: The child is crying in the village because he has lost his mother. Everyone is crying, and I am also crying with them. The chief's wife has given us food and we are eating together in the big house.

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Kafɔɔ Yesu ladiki kanya alɛ, “Nse kufĩ nfɛ kofe kamɛɛkɔ diɛ kolo lɛsɛɛ yi, mimbuɛ bilɛ kayi malɛ kɔsɛɛ.
translated: Jesus said to the young man, "There is one thing you lack, go and sell everything you have and give to the poor.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: “Ninfɛ di osuɔtɔ nwu nkpo lapɛ akunkyi di Ɔka nwu nkpo katũ aatikiti nwɔ lekoto osie kanya alɛ, ‘Wɛ otu fatɛɛ, mata koto nwu nkpo kumuu!’
translated: I cannot provide a translation of the text as it is in the Selee language, which I couldn't find any information on. If you can provide more context or details about the Selee language, I may be able to help you better.

ref: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
text: “Nfa nfɛ di Ɔka nwu nkpo madiki kanya alɛ, ‘Nwaako kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, di obe wɔ biɛnya biɛbla nle mmle biɛta ni babiloo ba mmle yɛntɛle onwii lɛma kamaa, ami biɛbla biɛta.’
translated: “When the death of a chief is announced in the town, they say, ‘The mighty tree has fallen, and all the little trees and leaves are withered, and the little rivers have dried up, and the little animals are scattered, and the little birds have flown away.’”

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Ninfɛ baayɔ nta be bɛɛkpɔtɔɔsa ni ku kɔfa lonle baata Yesu alɛ anyi nɛ. Kafɔɔ obe wɔ aatu aanyu nii, aasĩ mi onyi.
translated: The women went to the tomb early in the morning and did not find the body of Jesus there. They saw two angels sitting where the body had been, wearing shining clothes.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Aplakuo ɔlambu kplɛ onwii latoole alesaa di ntɛɛ nwu.
translated: The stranger's dog is barking loudly under the big tree.
